sense.definition: a classic poker game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two to six players

examples.x: Five-card draw is a great way to introduce new players to poker.
He won the pot in the last hand of five-card draw with a straight flush.
In five-card draw, players can discard any number of cards to improve their hand.
We decided to play five-card draw after dinner because it’s easy and fun.

sense.definition: a popular poker variant where players are dealt two private cards and five community cards, aiming to form the best five-card hand possible

examples.x: She won the tournament by playing a strong hand at Texas hold 'em and outsmarting her opponents.
Texas hold 'em is the most widely played poker game in casinos around the world.
He raised the bet after the flop in Texas hold 'em, hoping to bluff his opponents into folding.
The strategy in Texas hold 'em can change dramatically after the turn and river are revealed.

sense.definition: a poker game similar to Texas Hold'em, but players are dealt four private cards, and they must use exactly two of them in combination with three of the five community cards to make the best five-card hand

examples.x: I prefer Omaha hold 'em over Texas hold 'em because the additional hole cards make the game more exciting and unpredictable.
In Omaha hold 'em, players must use two of their hole cards and three community cards to make their final hand.
In Omaha hold 'em, it is essential to pay close attention to both your hole cards and the community cards to form a winning hand.
I find Omaha hold 'em challenging because there are a lot of possible hand combinations to consider.

sense.definition: a poker game where players are dealt seven cards, three face down and four face up, and they must use the best five-card hand to win

examples.x: After the fourth card was dealt face up, it was time for another round of betting in seven-card stud.
In seven-card stud, the visible cards give players clues about their opponents' hands.
Seven-card stud requires both skill and observation, as players need to assess the strength of their own hand and watch their opponents’ exposed cards.

sense.definition: a variation of seven-card stud poker, where the lowest five-card hand wins the pot
a variation of Texas hold 'em poker, where players receive three hole cards and discard one before the flop

examples.x: We played Pineapple poker last night, and it was exciting to see who would make the best use of their three hole cards.
He wasn’t sure which card to keep in Pineapple poker, as all three seemed useful for different reasons.
After the flop, she realized discarding that card in Pineapple poker had been a mistake, as it could have helped her make a straight.
They switched to Pineapple poker for the second half of the game, and everyone was excited to see how the extra card would affect the hands.

sense.definition: a type of poker game in which players use shared cards alongside their individual hole cards to form their hands

examples.x: In community card poker, players use their own cards and the shared cards to make the best hand.
Community card poker is exciting because everyone uses the same shared cards.
In community card poker, players watch the shared cards closely to decide their next move.
Players often bluff in community card poker, pretending their hand is stronger than it is.

sense.definition: a poker variant in which players are dealt a complete hand and then have the opportunity to exchange some or all of their cards for new ones in an attempt to improve their hand

examples.x: Draw poker requires players to think carefully about which cards to keep and which to replace.
She won the pot in draw poker with a straight after drawing two cards.
In a typical draw poker game, players are allowed to discard up to five cards.

sense.definition: a poker variant that is played with four cards, and the objective is to create the lowest possible hand with all cards of different suits and ranks

examples.x: After the first round of betting, players in badugi can exchange up to four cards to improve their hand.
The dealer dealt four cards to each player, and then they decided whether to exchange any cards during the badugi draw.
After the second draw, Jack realized that his hand in badugi was still far from the winning combination.

sense.definition: a card game where players aim to create three distinct hands from a standard deck, competing to form stronger combinations than their opponents
a card game where players receive seven cards and must create two poker hands: one five-card hand and one two-card hand

examples.x: Some players prefer double-hand poker for the extra challenge of handling two hands at once.
After the first round of betting in double-hand poker, players can make adjustments to their strategy depending on the strength of both hands.
The rules of double-hand poker are similar to regular poker, except players must manage two separate hands.
During the game of double-hand poker, he folded one hand but raised with the other to maximize his chances.
She managed to win both hands in double-hand poker, taking home the entire pot.

sense.definition: a type of poker game where players use real money to buy-in for chips, and they can leave the game and cash out their chips at any time

examples.x: After a few hands in the cash game, Sarah decided to buy in for more chips to continue playing.
John enjoys cash games because they offer more control, allowing him to manage his chips and leave when he's ready.
The dealer shuffled the deck and started the cash game, with each player placing their bets using real chips.
Many poker players prefer cash games because they can join or leave the table whenever they like.

sense.definition: a variant of poker where the objective is to make the lowest possible hand with no straights or flushes, and players can draw three times to improve their hands

examples.x: After each draw in deuce to seven triple draw, players can decide which cards to keep and which ones to discard.
He was hoping to improve his hand in the second round of deuce to seven triple draw, but he ended up with a higher pair.
The key to winning in deuce to seven triple draw is knowing when to draw and when to stay with your current hand.
She was in the lead for most of the game, but after the final draw in deuce to seven triple draw, her opponent won with a better low hand.

sense.definition: a poker variant where the objective is to make the lowest-ranking hand possible
a variant of Texas hold 'em played with a shortened deck containing only cards from six to Ace

examples.x: Many poker pros enjoy six-plus hold 'em because it requires a different approach than traditional Texas hold 'em.
Players new to six-plus hold 'em might be surprised at how much the odds shift due to the smaller deck size.
Aces in six-plus hold 'em can play both high and low, adding another layer of strategy to the game.

sense.definition: a classic poker game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two to eight players, in which each player is dealt a combination of face-up and face-down cards over several betting rounds

examples.x: He raised the bet in stud poker, hoping that his pair of kings would hold up against the others.
When playing stud poker, it’s important to remember which cards have already been dealt to other players.
I had a great run in stud poker last night, winning three consecutive hands.
The game of stud poker doesn’t use community cards, so everyone relies on their own hand.

sense.definition: a historic British gambling game similar to poker, where players are dealt three cards and bet on the strength of their hands
a variant of poker where the pot is split between the best high hand and the best low hand

examples.x: Practicing Omaha high-low poker helps improve decision-making skills for other card games.
Players often fold weak hands early in Omaha high-low poker to avoid losing chips.
Learning Omaha high-low poker can be challenging, but it is rewarding once the rules are clear.
